architecture_fit.note: Literally recomputes Q Kᵀ/√d in NumPy; no model/transformer delta from base_model.py, so it tautologically matches but does not address the mechanistic question.

baseline_comparison.note: Beats the unscaled strawman (lift=1.0), but that baseline is supplied by the goal infra; the attempt itself does no ablation of the √d term.

faithfulness.note: No model in the loop, no ablation/causal check, and the README does not propose what such a check would look like.

operating_range.note: Perfect across the full d_head sweep 8→128, though exactness is trivial since it is the literal formula.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Hand-built and deterministic, but it is the target formula itself rather than a hand-set circuit demonstrating understanding.

visual_judgement.note: app.py fails the boot-check: ImportError on `latest_run`, and demo.launch() sits at module level inside the Blocks context.

visualisation_rationale.note: A single heatmap of an exact reconstruction with no true/pred comparison and no scale-error emphasis fails to make the √d claim checkable.

notes: The attempt hand-codes the exact target formula Q Kᵀ/√d in NumPy, yielding perfect benchmark metrics (reconstruction_fidelity=1.0) but contributing no transformer-like mechanism, no ablation, and no faithfulness evidence — it is a tautological non-answer to the interpretability question. The Gradio app additionally fails its boot-check (ImportError on `latest_run`; demo.launch() at module level), so the visualisation is unusable. Worst of architecture/baseline/faithfulness drives a fail despite the perfect automated score.
